title: Task 19: Add Security Considerations section to moat-spec.md (DQ-2, Panel)
description:
Goal: Insert a '## Security Considerations' section before '## OWASP Alignment' covering revocation propagation worst case (96h), replay attack scope, trust decision attack surface, and lockfile integrity precision note.

File: moat-spec.md (~line 880, before OWASP Alignment section)

Depends on: Task 18 (moat-1fi)

What to do: See plan docs/plans/2026-04-13-panel-fixes-implementation.md Task 19.
Pre-condition: grep -n '^## OWASP Alignment' moat-spec.md → confirms insertion point exists.

Use old_string = '---\n\n## OWASP Alignment' as the insertion anchor.

The new section contains four subsections:
1. Revocation Propagation Worst Case: 96h for default-expiry, 28h for 4h-expiry registries
2. Replay Attack Scope: informative
3. Trust Decision Attack Surface: TOFU for manually-added registries
4. Lockfile Integrity: not MAC-protected; detection via moat-verify; precision note — does NOT detect targeted tampering where attacker also updates lockfile hashes

Full insertion text in plan Task 19 Step 1 (lines 1534-1573).

Success criteria:
- grep -n 'Security Considerations' moat-spec.md → passes
- grep -n '96 hours|96h' moat-spec.md → passes
- grep -n '28h|28 hours' moat-spec.md → passes
- grep -n 'Revocation Propagation|Replay Attack Scope|Trust Decision Attack|Lockfile Integrity' moat-spec.md → passes

title: Task 18: Add DQ-4/DQ-5 Crawl Optimization and Manifest Size to registry-action.md
description:
Goal: Insert two informative sections — Crawl Optimization and Manifest Size — after the Trust Tier Determination section in registry-action.md.

File: specs/registry-action.md (after Trust Tier Determination section, before Per-Item Canonical Payload)

Depends on: Task 17 (moat-4tq)

What to do: See plan docs/plans/2026-04-13-panel-fixes-implementation.md Task 18.
Note: Task 14 has already changed the Trust Tier Determination section ending. By the time this runs, the section ends with 'Clients MUST NOT hard-block on attestation_hash_mismatch alone...\n\n---\n\n## Per-Item Canonical Payload'.

Use old_string = '---\n\n## Per-Item Canonical Payload' as the insertion point.

Insert two sections before Per-Item Canonical Payload:

## Crawl Optimization (Informative)
  - Skip re-signing when content_hash unchanged AND OIDC identity matches
  - Identity check before reuse (normative guard): MUST verify OIDC identity still matches registry_signing_profile
  - Reuse criteria table with 3 rows

## Manifest Size (Informative)
  - Most registries 100-1000 items; ~300KB-600KB uncompressed
  - SHOULD emit ETag header
  - Jitter for crawl schedules (±1 hour)
  - Delta-sync and sharding deferred to post-v1.0

Full insertion text in plan Task 18 Step 1 (lines 1462-1487).

Success criteria:
- grep -n 'ETag|jitter|Crawl Optimization|OIDC identity check' specs/registry-action.md → passes
- grep -n 'manifest size|delta.sync|sharding' specs/registry-action.md → passes

title: Task 5: Rewrite TV-09 and TV-10 for reject-all symlink behavior
description:

File: reference/generate_test_vectors.py (functions vector_09() and vector_10(), lines ~220-255)

Depends on: Task 4 (moat-ob5)

What to do: See plan docs/plans/2026-04-13-panel-fixes-implementation.md Task 5.
Step 1: Replace entire vector_09() with a must_error=True dict. New content:
  - id: 9, title: 'Internal symlink — MUST error', section: '7.3'
  - input_description: describes internal symlink rejected by reject-all policy
  - content_hash: 'ERROR: Symlink rejected — content MUST be rejected as unpublishable'
  - must_error: True
  - note: moat_hash.py raises ValueError for any symlink
Step 2: Replace entire vector_10() with a must_error=True dict (external symlink variant, same structure).
Full replacement code is in the plan Task 5 Steps 1 and 2.

Success criteria:

- grep -A3 'TV-10' reference/generate_test_vectors.py | grep 'must_error' → passes

title: Task 20: Add test vector normative authority statement to spec and moat_hash.py (Panel)
description:
Goal: Declare test vectors as normative authority in moat-spec.md and demote moat_hash.py to informative; update moat_hash.py docstring accordingly.

Files:
- moat-spec.md (Reference implementations section, ~lines 154-169)
- reference/moat_hash.py (module docstring, lines 1-10)

Depends on: Task 19 (moat-0gm)

What to do: See plan docs/plans/2026-04-13-panel-fixes-implementation.md Task 20.

Step 1 - moat-spec.md: Replace the moat_hash.py entry in the reference implementations section.
  OLD: **[reference/moat_hash.py]...** — Python reference implementation...Conforming implementations...MUST produce identical output...
  NEW: Two entries:
    - generate_test_vectors.py — **Normative.** Test vectors are the authoritative specification; implementation non-conforming if it disagrees with a test vector.
    - moat_hash.py — **Informative.** Useful as starting point; NOT the normative specification.
Full replacement text in plan Task 20 Step 1 (lines 1608-1611).

Step 2 - moat_hash.py: Replace module docstring.
  OLD: 'MOAT v0.4.0 — Content Hash Reference Implementation\n\nThis is the normative reference...'
  NEW: 'MOAT v0.4.0 — Content Hash Reference Implementation (Informative)\n\nThis script is an informative reference implementation. The normative authority for correct output is the test vector suite...'
Full replacement text in plan Task 20 Step 2 (lines 1631-1647).

Success criteria:
- grep -n 'test vector.*normative|normative.*test vector|authoritative.*test vector' moat-spec.md → passes
- grep -n 'reference implementation.*informative|informative.*reference implementation' moat-spec.md → passes
- grep -A5 'Content Hash Reference Implementation' reference/moat_hash.py | grep -i 'informative' → passes
- grep -n 'normative authority' reference/moat_hash.py → passes

title: Task 17: Add DQ-1 TUF staleness model to moat-spec.md (DQ-1, DQ-3, DQ-6)
description:
Goal: Replace the Freshness Guarantee and Replay Scope section with the TUF-model text (72h default, expires field, fetched_at clock, failed refresh rule). Also update the stale manifest bullet in Conforming Clients from 24h to the new model.

File: moat-spec.md (Freshness Guarantee and Replay Scope section, lines 361-382; and Conforming Clients stale manifest bullet ~lines 238-241)

Depends on: Task 16 (moat-j4g) — spec changes committed.

What to do: See plan docs/plans/2026-04-13-panel-fixes-implementation.md Task 17.

Step 1: Replace the entire 'Freshness Guarantee and Replay Scope' section (old_string: full 21-line section from '### Freshness Guarantee' through the final paragraph). New section adopts TUF model:
  - expires field (registry sets); 72h default from fetched_at (client enforces)
  - Staleness checked at install time only
  - Failed refresh MUST NOT reset staleness clock
  - 72h calibration explanation (weekend test)
  - Air-gapped environments note
  - No separate revocation endpoint note (DQ-3)
  - No per-entry expiry note (DQ-6)
Full replacement text in plan Task 17 Step 1 (lines 1356-1383).

Step 2: Update the stale manifest bullet in Conforming Clients section from 24h to the new model.
  OLD: MUST NOT use cached manifest...exceeds configurable staleness threshold (default: 24 hours)...
  NEW: MUST NOT use cached manifest...current time exceeds expires (if present) or exceeds fetched_at + 72 hours (if expires absent)...failed refresh MUST NOT reset staleness clock.
Full replacement text in plan Task 17 Step 2 (lines 1401-1405).

Success criteria:
- grep -n '72 hours|72h|expires' moat-spec.md | grep -v '^Binary' → passes (72h and expires present)
- grep -n 'fetched_at|install time|not continuously' moat-spec.md → passes
- grep -n 'Failed refresh MUST NOT reset|MUST NOT reset.*staleness' moat-spec.md → passes

title: Task 11: Add Non-Interactive Client Behavior bullet to moat-spec.md (SC-3)
description:
Goal: Insert a normative '**Non-interactive client behavior**' bullet in the Conforming Clients section with a four-row trigger condition table.

File: moat-spec.md (Conforming Clients section, around lines 228-248)

Depends on: Task 10 (moat-6ga)

What to do: See plan docs/plans/2026-04-13-panel-fixes-implementation.md Task 11.
Pre-condition: grep -n '**Lineage model**' moat-spec.md and confirm it returns a result.

Use old_string = end of revocation bullet ('  the protocol level.\n- **Lineage model**...') and insert the Non-Interactive bullet between them. The new bullet covers four trigger conditions in a table:
  | TOFU signing profile acceptance | Exit non-zero |
  | registry_signing_profile change | Exit non-zero |
  | Publisher revocation | Exit non-zero |
  | Manifest staleness exceeded | Exit non-zero |

The bullet must state: exit non-zero AND machine-distinguishable error signal AND failure class distinguishable by automated caller. References DQ-8 for pre-approval mechanism.

Full text is in the plan Task 11 Step 1 (lines 945-977).

Success criteria:
- grep -n 'non-interactive|Non-Interactive|Non-interactive' moat-spec.md | grep -v '^Binary' → passes (after edit)
- grep -n 'machine-distinguishable' moat-spec.md → passes
- grep -n 'TOFU signing profile|registry_signing_profile change|Manifest staleness' moat-spec.md → passes

title: Task 10: Add Version Transition section to moat-spec.md (SC-2)
description:
Goal: Insert a new '## Version Transition' section after '## Trust Model' and before '## Fork and Lineage Handling'.

File: moat-spec.md (~line 426)

Depends on: Task 9 (moat-cn0)

What to do: See plan docs/plans/2026-04-13-panel-fixes-implementation.md Task 10.
The section covers:
1. Ordering: content_hash MUST be checked BEFORE _version field (prevents TOCTOU race)
2. Grace period: 6 months for _version transitions; clients MUST accept both old and new values during the window
3. What counts as a _version bump: additive-only changes MAY use forward-compatibility; breaking changes REQUIRE a bump

Use old_string = '---\n\n## Fork and Lineage Handling' (the separator and the next section header) to insert the new section between the end of Trust Model and Fork and Lineage Handling.

Full section text is in the plan Task 10 Step 1 (lines 878-909).

Pre-condition verification: grep -n '^## Trust Model' moat-spec.md and grep -n '^## Fork and Lineage Handling' moat-spec.md both return results.

Success criteria:
- grep -n 'Version Transition' moat-spec.md → passes
- grep -n 'MUST be checked BEFORE' moat-spec.md → passes
- grep -n '6 months' moat-spec.md → passes
- grep -n 'TOCTOU' moat-spec.md → passes

title: Task 14: Strengthen Hash Mismatch paragraph in registry-action.md (SC-6)
description:
Goal: Replace the non-normative 'Hash mismatch' paragraph with a normative 'Hash mismatch (normative)' block that clearly states MUST downgrade requirements.

File: specs/registry-action.md (Trust Tier Determination section, ~line 106)

Depends on: Task 13 (moat-7vm)

What to do: See plan docs/plans/2026-04-13-panel-fixes-implementation.md Task 14.

Replace the existing '**Hash mismatch:**' paragraph with '**Hash mismatch (normative):**' block. The new block has four numbered requirements:
1. Item indexed at registry's computed hash (not publisher's)
2. Trust tier set to Signed regardless of Rekor verification
3. attestation_hash_mismatch: true MUST be recorded in manifest
4. Action MUST log warning with item, registry hash, and publisher hash

Also adds:
- 'Ambiguous case' paragraph (matching hash but old attestation — still Dual-Attested; MUST NOT set attestation_hash_mismatch: true)
- 'Conforming client behavior on attestation_hash_mismatch' paragraph

Full replacement text in plan Task 14 Step 1 (lines 1210-1219).

Success criteria:

title: Task 13: Add SC-5 Revocation Archival section and fetched_at to moat-spec.md
description:
Goal: Add revocation archival semantics (tombstone rule, 180-day retention, lockfile authority) and add fetched_at field to the lockfile schema.

File: moat-spec.md (Conforming Clients section and Lockfile schema section)

Depends on: Task 12 (moat-35w)

What to do: See plan docs/plans/2026-04-13-panel-fixes-implementation.md Task 13.

Step 1: Insert Revocation archival bullet immediately before '**Lineage model**'. The bullet covers:
  - 180-day minimum retention period for revocation entries
  - Lockfile is authoritative for pruned revocations (client MUST NOT remove revoked_hashes entries)
  - Tombstone rule: revocation-tombstones.json file in moat-registry branch; content_hashes that were revoked and pruned MUST NOT reappear in content[] array
  - 180-day calibration explanation
Full text in plan Task 13 Step 1 (lines 1084-1097).

Step 2: Update lockfile minimum structure JSON to add 'registries' top-level key with fetched_at field. Also add registries and registries[url].fetched_at rows to the lockfile field table.

Step 3: Add upgrade path note: if a client reads a lockfile without 'registries' key, SHOULD initialize on next successful fetch.

Success criteria:
- grep -n 'Revocation Archival|180.day|retention period|tombstone' moat-spec.md → passes
- grep -n 'revocation-tombstones.json' moat-spec.md → passes
- grep -n 'lockfile.*authoritative|authoritative.*lockfile' moat-spec.md → passes
- grep -n '"fetched_at"' moat-spec.md → passes (in JSON example)
- grep -n 'fetched_at.*RFC 3339|RFC 3339.*fetched_at' moat-spec.md → passes (in field table)
- grep -n '"registries"' moat-spec.md → passes (top-level key in JSON example)

title: Task 6: Create reference/test_normalization.py with TV-17 through TV-22
description:
Goal: Create a new integration test file testing BOM stripping, CRLF normalization, and binary classification via real filesystem I/O.

File to create: reference/test_normalization.py

Depends on: Task 5 (moat-2g6) — reference implementation state is stable.

What to do: Create the complete file as specified in plan docs/plans/2026-04-13-panel-fixes-implementation.md Task 6 Step 1. The full file content is in the plan (lines 348-668). The file includes:
  - TV-17: CRLF normalization (.md file)
  - TV-18: UTF-8 BOM stripping (.py file)
  - TV-19: Extensionless dotfile treated as binary (.gitignore)
  - TV-20: NUL byte forces binary classification (.json)
  - TV-21: CR at chunk boundary (65534 'a' bytes + CRLF split across chunks)
  - TV-22: Lone CR at EOF
Each test writes real files to tempfile.TemporaryDirectory and calls moat_hash.content_hash().

Success criteria:
- python3 reference/test_normalization.py 2>&1 | grep 'All 6 normalization tests passed' → passes
- python3 reference/test_normalization.py 2>&1 | grep 'FAIL' → grep exits non-zero (no failures)

title: Task 1: Fix content_hash_directory() to sha256sum format
description:
Goal: Rewrite content_hash_directory() in generate_test_vectors.py to use sha256sum format, matching moat_hash.py.

File: reference/generate_test_vectors.py (lines 27-56)

What to do: Replace the content_hash_directory() function body with sha256sum format implementation. See plan: docs/plans/2026-04-13-panel-fixes-implementation.md Task 1, Step 1. The new function builds a manifest as sha256sum-format lines ('{hash}  {path}\n' per entry), NFC-normalizes paths, checks for NFC collisions, sorts by NFC-normalized UTF-8 byte order, and returns 'sha256:{sha256_hex(manifest)}'. Requires unicodedata import.

Success criteria:
- python3 reference/generate_test_vectors.py 2>&1 | grep 'TV-03' -A3 | grep 'content_hash' → shows sha256:354fd3217a271a7b3e8862ece0984e05162cd96d11ebaee78e277babf18d81f3
- python3 reference/generate_test_vectors.py 2>&1 | grep 'deterministic' → passes
